title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Proxy Trust, and Client Identity · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A backend pairing room predicts runtime, request, middleware, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first request authority and trusted-proxy decision record for request targets, host authority, proxy trust, and client identity. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of request authority and trusted-proxy decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.
Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.
Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Express Request Field Meaning
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 3631 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Express Request Field Meaning.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret method, url, originalurl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Express Request Field Meaning, keep Express Request Field Meaning connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A workshop team handles case 7253 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record bounded route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, personal data, or unbounded query values.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Authority Redaction Evidence symptom.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Authority Redaction Evidence happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Authority Redaction Evidence condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Authority Redaction Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Authority Redaction Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop team handles case 2396 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record bounded route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, personal data, or unbounded query values.
